standard_description: Understand that restricting a trigonometric function to a domain on which it is always increasing or always decreasing allows its inverse to be constructed.
subject: Math

problems.text:
Find two values where
$${\mathrm{cos}x=-1}$$
.
What is
$${\mathrm{arccos}(-1)}$$
?

Problem 1
Solve for the general solution:
$${3\mathrm{tan}^2x-3=0}$$
$${2\mathrm{cos}^2(x)-\sqrt3 \mathrm{cos}x=0}$$
$${2\mathrm{cos}^2x+\mathrm{cos}x-1=0}$$
Problem 2
Solve for the domain
$${0 \leq \theta \leq 2\pi}$$
:
$${2\mathrm{sin}^2\theta+\mathrm{cos}\theta\mathrm{sin}^2\theta=0}$$

Find points of intersection of the following functions:
$${f(x)=2\mathrm{sin}x}$$
$${g(x)={-\sqrt3\over 2}}$$

Problem 1
Below are sets of three equations that are equivalent. Find
$$x$$
for each set of equations.
###TABLE0###
Problem 2
Solve the following equations. Write your answers in radians.
$${\mathrm{sin}x={1\over2}}$$
$${\mathrm{cos}^{-1}\left({\sqrt2\over2}\right)=x}$$
$${\mathrm{arcsin}(0.35)=x}$$

Problem 1
Below is a student's answer (in three steps, left to right) to the following problem:
Graph the inverse of the function
$${y=\mathrm{sin}x}$$
.
###IMAGE0###
What did the student do in each step?
What did the student do well?
How can the student's answer be improved?
Problem 2
What is the solution to the system of:
$$\left\{\begin{matrix}
y=\mathrm{cos}x\\ y=-1
\end{matrix}\right.$$

Solve the following equations for all values
$${0\leq x\leq2\pi}$$
.
$${3\mathrm{sin}\theta+2\mathrm{sin}^2\theta=-1}$$
$${\mathrm{sin}\theta\mathrm{cos}\theta-{1\over2}\mathrm{sin}\theta=0}$$
